payload.text: I'd like to make a website for "Hektic Hub" where my wife and I display all of our offers. there would be "services," "entertainment," and "products." In services, there is a company for "Premiere Weddings Services & Videography" for wedding videography inquiries, "Pocketopia" for app development inquires and our app portfolio, "Twizted Images Cinema" for music videos, events and company commercials, and "New Church of Orion" which is a religious organization that I had created. "Entertainment" will have our amazon prime tv series "Band Wars" with a Youtube trailer link, links to the show, and "Hektic Nation" which is our podcast and Youtube series, and "Cryptamnesia" for our Industrial metal band. "Products" will have "Acropolis Apparel & Accessories" for custom leather jackets and jewelry,  my "Rise of Darkus" book series store link and bio. Make the main hub page sleek and professional - almost futuristic, but then theme each topic within each category to what it is. the color theme is red, white and black.

**Conceptualizing Website Structure**

I'm currently focused on the overarching structure and visual language for "Hektic Hub."  My goal is a professional, sleek, and futuristic design, using a red, white, and black color palette. The hub will link to distinct sections: "Premiere Weddings Services & Videography," "Pocketopia" (app development), and "Twizted Images."


**Mapping Content Categories**

I've been drilling down on content categorization for the Hektic Hub.  I've fleshed out specific areas: "Cinema" including projects like music videos, and other key sections.  I'm also considering unique branding for each category. For instance, the industrial metal band Cryptamnesia will have a different UI feel than Premiere Weddings. I am envisioning a grid or tabbed interface.


**Designing Functional Components**

I'm now deep-diving into the actual React component design. I'm focusing on key elements: a Header, HubGrid for navigation, BrandDetail for deep dives, and a Hero section for impact. I've also begun to sketch out UI considerations for each category, ranging from elegant Premiere Weddings to glitchy Cryptamnesia. I'm thinking about integrating an "Assistant" feature using Gemini.


**Integrating Tech Specs**

I'm now incorporating specific technical elements into my design. I'm focusing on named imports, using `createRoot` for React 18, and implementing Tailwind via CDN. I'll utilize `useState` to manage active categories and selected brands. To address the "futuristic" versus specific-theme challenge, I'll leverage a `DynamicThemeWrapper` for shifting accent colors. I've decided to employ Lucide-react for high-quality SVG icons and plan to use Picsum with filters or descriptive placeholders for images.

**Exploring File Uploads**

I'm currently focused on the practical implementation of local file uploads for the "Admin Menu." Moving beyond just image URLs is the priority. My efforts are on integrating this functionality for both images and videos directly into the admin interface. I'm exploring the storage implications, especially how to replace `localStorage` effectively.


**Integrating File Uploads**

I've made progress on the technical implementation of the file upload feature. I've begun modifying `AdminMenu.tsx` to include the file input fields for both images and videos. A helper function to convert files to base64 strings is in the works, necessary for persisting data in `localStorage`.  I'm also considering updates to the `Brand` type to accommodate video data.  My focus is on a seamless integration for admins to upload and preview both image and video files.


**Enhancing Upload Mechanics**

I'm now integrating drag-and-drop file pickers with progress icons. Real-time previews are essential, and the `BrandModal` will include a video player for uploaded videos. However, I've hit a storage snag with `localStorage`. Large base64 videos won't work, so I'm considering using IndexedDB for larger files. For now, I'll limit file sizes and include a warning, while starting to implement the file-to-data-url system.


**Rethinking Storage Strategy**

I've shifted my focus to a more streamlined approach for handling uploaded files. Instead of converting files to base64 and persisting them directly in `localStorage`, I'll integrate `FileReader` to update the `brand` object.  The `types.ts` file needs modification to include `customVideoUrl` and `customImageUrl`.  My goal is a responsive Admin Menu that handles video previews with a command center layout. I'm also planning to use standard request logic, assuming the environment will handle data.


**Defining Data Structures**
